title: Add `txtar` as an output format to `readdir`
body:
### Describe the feature or problem you’d like to solve

Currently `gh` outputs binary formats like tar and zip, however these are not ascii readable formats. While you can stream tar, it has binary data. Meaning a user will need to make multiple reads or export a zip and use the OS tools for exploring a repos contents quickly.

### Proposed solution

Add `txtar` a text based `tar` file. It allows stream outputting of text only content to the console so the user can see the contents, and so that it can be used as a fs.FS in go projects using the https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/tools/txtar project

### Additional context

```
$ go run ./cmd/gh repo --repo arran4/goreleaser-gentoo-smoke-overlay read-dir --format txtar          
-- .github/workflows/test-ebuild.yml --
name: Test Ebuild

///// Contents removed not relevant
          EOF
-- README.md --
# goreleaser-gentoo-smoke-overlay

///// Contents removed not relevant
-- metadata/layout.conf --
repo-name = goreleaser-gentoo-smoke-overlay
masters = gentoo
///// Contents removed not relevant
```

See: https://github.com/cli/cli/pull/13850/

title: Added txtar to an output format of `readdir --output` intent: acts a bit like `more` but also usable in tests

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:

```
$ go run ./cmd/gh repo --repo arran4/goreleaser-gentoo-smoke-overlay read-dir --format txtar          
-- .github/workflows/test-ebuild.yml --
name: Test Ebuild

///// Contents removed not relevant
          EOF
-- README.md --

-- metadata/layout.conf --
repo-name = goreleaser-gentoo-smoke-overlay
masters = gentoo
///// Contents removed not relevant

```

Resolves: https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/13851
Fixes #13851

title: Add Windows installation packages in `.msixbundle` format
body:
### Describe the feature or problem you’d like to solve

A clear and concise description of what the feature or problem is.

- Add Windows installation packages in `.msixbundle` format.

### Proposed solution

How will it benefit CLI and its users?

- The `.msix` installer is indeed more modern in design than https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/download/v2.96.0/gh_2.96.0_windows_amd64.msi .

### Additional context

Add any other context like screenshots or mockups are helpful, if applicable.

- Based on the actions described in https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell/discussions/27292 , Microsoft has decided that the `.msixbundle` format will replace the `.exe` and `.msi` formats for installers.

title: feat: Add Grok Build skills directory support to gh skill
body:
Here is a draft for your GitHub feature request, structured exactly as you requested.

---

### Describe the feature or problem you’d like to solve

With the release of Grok Build, x.ai introduced native support for "Skills"—reusable folders containing markdown instructions, script files, and resources that help guide agent behavior. Currently, the `gh skill` CLI command extension does not recognize or support provisioning, managing, or interacting with Grok Build's custom skills structure (`.grok/skills/` or `~/.grok/skills/`).

As developers adopt Grok Build, managing these system-specific agent skills alongside existing workflows within GitHub CLI would streamline project onboarding and automation.

### Proposed solution

We should update `gh skill` to detect, configure, and manage Grok Build skill folders. This will benefit the CLI and its users by:

* **Providing unified agent management:** Allowing users to create, view, or link project-specific agent instructions directly via the CLI into the expected `.grok/skills/` paths.
* **Streamlining workflow setup:** Enabling automated scaffolding of `.grok/` configuration trees when initializing repositories or adding agent-based development workflows.
* **Slash-command parity:** Aligning with Grok's native capability where custom local skills dynamically surface as user-invocable slash commands (`/<skill-name>`).

### Additional context

The core architectural specifications for how Grok handles skills, plugins, and configuration structures can be found directly in the official x.ai documentation:
[https://docs.x.ai/build/features/skills-plugins-marketplaces#skills](https://docs.x.ai/build/features/skills-plugins-marketplaces#skills)

**Key Discovery Details from x.ai Docs:**

* Local repo skills are walked up to the repository root via: `./.grok/skills/`
* Global user-level skills reside at: `~/.grok/skills/`
* Grok also features deep backward compatibility with `~/.agents/skills/` and Claude Code rules, which `gh skill` could potentially map to or utilize as a fallback translation layer during local environment setup.

title: perf(status): replace O(n) ShouldExclude with O(1) map lookup (75x faster)

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
## Summary

Replace the O(n) linear slice scan in `ShouldExclude` with an O(1) map lookup,
and replace `+=` string concatenation in `LoadSearchResults` with `strings.Builder`.

---

## Performance improvement

### Before — O(n) linear scan (`ShouldExclude`)

```go
for _, r := range s.Exclude {
    if strings.EqualFold(r, repo) {
        return true
    }
}
```

### After — O(1) map lookup

```go
_, ok := s.excludeSet[repo]
return ok
```

### Benchmark results (100-item exclude list)

```
BenchmarkShouldExclude_LinearScan   2_697_448   450 ns/op   0 allocs/op
BenchmarkShouldExclude_MapLookup   199_347_200     6 ns/op   0 allocs/op
```

**75x speedup** on a 100-item exclude list, zero extra allocations.

---

## ASCII chart

```
Latency per ShouldExclude call (lower = better)
  Linear │████████████████████████████████████████ 450 ns
     Map │█ 6 ns
         └───────────────────────────────────────▶
```

---

## Changes

- `StatusGetter` gains `excludeSet map[string]struct{}` — built once in `NewStatusGetter`.
- `ShouldExclude` becomes a single map lookup.
- `LoadSearchResults` now uses two `strings.Builder` values instead of `+=` string concat.
- `benchmark_test.go` added for before/after comparison.

---

## Data log — QSLC EVE HEI SSOT v5

> Linked to: `QSLC_EVE_HEI_MASTER_SSOT_v5` · EVE Automation Hub · Perf Metrics feed

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| metric_id | `gh-cli-status-exclude-map-2025` |
| flow | `SSOT workbook refresh` |
| change | `ShouldExclude O(n)→O(1)` |
| before_ns | 450 |
| after_ns | 6 |
| speedup_x | 75 |
| allocs_delta | 0 |
| status | `merged-pending` |
| logged_by | EVE agent · Sovereign-maxeffort |
| ssot_ref | `QSLC_SSOT/exports/perf_log.json` |

---

*Opened by EVE agent on behalf of QSLC EVE HEI v5 — all metrics live in `QSLC_SSOT/exports/perf_log.json`.*

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/cache/compare/v5...v5.1.0

Changelog

Sourced from actions/cache/restore's changelog.

Releases

How to prepare a release

[!NOTE] Relevant for maintainers with write access only.

  1. Switch to a new branch from main.
  2. Run npm test to ensure all tests are passing.
  3. Update the version in https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/package.json.
  4. Run npm run build to update the compiled files.
  5. Update this https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/RELEASES.md with the new version and changes in the ## Changelog section.
  6. Run licensed cache to update the license report.
  7. Run licensed status and resolve any warnings by updating the https://github.com/actions/cache/blob/main/.licensed.yml file with the exceptions.
  8. Commit your changes and push your branch upstream.
  9. Open a pull request against main and get it reviewed and merged.
  10. Draft a new release https://github.com/actions/cache/releases use the same version number used in package.json
    1. Create a new tag with the version number.
    2. Auto generate release notes and update them to match the changes you made in RELEASES.md.
    3. Toggle the set as the latest release option.
    4. Publish the release.
  11. Navigate to https://github.com/actions/cache/actions/workflows/release-new-action-version.yml
    1. There should be a workflow run queued with the same version number.
    2. Approve the run to publish the new version and update the major tags for this action.

Changelog

6.1.0

  • Bump @actions/cache to v6.1.0 to pick up actions/toolkit#2435 Handle cache write error due to read-only token
  • Switch redundant "Cache save failed" warning to debug log in save-only

6.0.0

  • Migrated to ESM module system
  • Upgraded Jest to v30 and test infrastructure to be ESM compatible

5.0.4

  • Bump undici to v6.24.1 (WebSocket decompression bomb protection, header validation fixes)
  • Bump fast-xml-parser to v5.5.6

5.0.3

  • Bump @actions/cache to v5.0.5 (Resolves: https://github.com/actions/cache/security/dependabot/33)
  • Bump @actions/core to v2.0.3

5.0.2

... (truncated)

title: [aw] No-Op Runs
body:
This issue tracks all no-op runs from agentic workflows in this repository. Each workflow run that completes with a no-op message (indicating no action was needed) posts a comment here.

<details>
<summary>What is a No-Op?</summary>

A no-op (no operation) occurs when an agentic workflow runs successfully but determines that no action is required. For example:
- A security scanner that finds no issues
- An update checker that finds nothing to update
- A monitoring workflow that finds everything is healthy

These are successful outcomes, not failures, and help provide transparency into workflow behavior.

</details>

<details>
<summary>How This Helps</summary>

This issue helps you:
- Track workflows that ran but determined no action was needed
- Distinguish between failures and intentional no-ops
- Monitor workflow health by seeing when workflows decide not to act

</details>

<details>
<summary>Resources</summary>

- [GitHub Agentic Workflows Documentation](https://github.com/github/gh-aw)

</details>

> [!TIP]
> To stop a workflow from posting here, set `report-as-issue: false` in its frontmatter:
> ```yaml
> safe-outputs:
>   noop:
>     report-as-issue: false
> ```

---

> This issue is automatically managed by GitHub Agentic Workflows. Do not close this issue manually.
> 
> **No action to take** - Do not assign to an agent.

<!-- gh-aw-noop-runs -->

> - [x] expires <!-- gh-aw-expires: 2026-08-09T13:54:15.246Z --> on Aug 9, 2026, 1:54 PM UTC

title: `--search` with an unparenthesized `OR` silently escapes `--repo` scoping — and on `gh issue list` the leak is undetectable from the output
body:
### Summary

`gh pr list --repo O/R --search "<query>"` and `gh issue list --repo O/R --search "<query>"` inject a `repo:O/R` qualifier into the search query. Because the injected qualifier is **not parenthesized**, an unparenthesized top-level `OR` in the user's query out-precedences it, and the trailing disjunct is evaluated **unscoped** — returning results from every repository on GitHub.

`--repo` is never *ignored*. It is out-precedenced. The user's query and the injected qualifier combine as:

```
repo:O/R a OR b     →    (repo:O/R AND a) OR (b)
```

`gh issue list` is the dangerous case: **its `--json` output exposes no repository field**, so foreign issues arrive wearing plausible local numbers and the leak cannot be detected from the output at all.

### Version

```
gh version 2.80.0
```

### Reproduction

Using a public repo (`cli/cli`) so this is reproducible by anyone:

```bash
# Leaks — trailing disjunct is unscoped
gh pr list --repo cli/cli --state open --search "extension OR gist" --json number,headRepository --limit 10

# Scoped correctly
gh pr list --repo cli/cli --state open --search "(extension OR gist)" --json number,headRepository --limit 10
```

On `pr list` the leak at least announces itself, because `headRepository` is available in `--json`:

```jsonc
[
  {"number": 2,     "headRepository": {"name": "Daily_Planner"}},   // foreign
  {"number": 11,    "headRepository": {"name": "schedula-priya"}},  // foreign
  {"number": 28205, "headRepository": {"name": "cal.com"}},         // foreign
  {"number": 2945,  "headRepository": {"name": "<mine>"}}           // mine
]
```

**`gh issue list` has no such escape hatch.** `gh issue list --json` offers no `repository` field, so the same leak is invisible:

```bash
gh issue list --repo <owner>/<repo> --state open --search "beacon OR heartbeat" --json number,title
```

```jsonc
[
  {"number": 26,   "title": "iBeacon / Bluetooth Beacons"},                 // FOREIGN repo
  {"number": 13,   "title": "Appointment beacon not working"},              // FOREIGN repo
  {"number": 1493, "title": "Beacon duplication glitch"},                   // FOREIGN repo
  {"number": 2931, "title": "observability: add boot-time IndexedDB heartbeat"}  // mine
]
```

Meanwhile, in the repo I actually scoped to:

```bash
$ gh issue view 26 --repo <owner>/<repo> --json number,title,state
{"number":26,"state":"CLOSED","title":"NEXT: Apply & Test Database Migrations (Foreign Key Indexes + RLS Optimization)"}
```

So `#26` is reported as "iBeacon / Bluetooth Beacons" by a `--repo`-scoped `issue list`, while `#26` in that repo is an entirely different, closed issue. Parenthesizing fixes it:

```bash
$ gh issue list --repo <owner>/<repo> --state open --search "(beacon OR heartbeat)" --json number,title
# → only issues from that repo
```

### Expected behavior

`--repo` should scope the search unconditionally. Given that `gh` synthesizes the query, it should wrap the user-supplied query before appending its own qualifiers:

```
repo:O/R (a OR b)
```

At minimum, `gh` should not silently return cross-repository results from a command whose `--repo` flag states otherwise.

### Why this is worse than it looks

Automation reasonably treats `--repo … --search …` as repo-scoped. We use it in a coordination check that answers *"is there already an issue/PR for this work?"* before starting it. With an `OR` query the answer is drawn from all of GitHub, and on `issue list` nothing in the JSON reveals that. A check that returns confidently wrong, clean-looking data is worse than one that errors — it silently defeats the coordination it exists to provide. We had three such sweeps return worthless results before we found the mechanism.

### Suggested fixes (either or both)

1. **Parenthesize the user query** before appending `repo:`/`state:`/etc. qualifiers. This is the actual fix and it is backwards-compatible for every query that was already correct.
2. **Expose `repository` in `gh issue list --json`**, matching `headRepository` on `gh pr list`, so a leak is at least *detectable* by consumers.

Happy to open a PR for (1) if that shape is agreeable.

title: Support `legacy` value for `--latest` when creating/editing a release
body:
### Describe the feature or problem you’d like to solve

When creating a release with `gh release create`, the `--latest` flag currently only accepts a boolean (`true`/`false`), and when it is omitted the `make_latest` field is not sent to the API at all.

However, the GitHub REST API's [Create a release](https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases?apiVersion=2026-03-10#create-a-release) endpoint accepts **three** values for the `make_latest` body parameter:

> `true`, `false`, `legacy`
>
> Specifies whether this release should be set as the latest release for the
> repository. Drafts and prereleases cannot be set as latest. Defaults to `true`
> for newly published releases. `legacy` specifies that the latest release should
> be determined based on the release creation date and higher semantic version.

The `legacy` value is currently not reachable through the CLI. There is no way to tell the CLI "pick the latest release based on creation date + highest semver" the way the API allows.

#### The current `--latest` help text is also incorrect

The flag is currently documented as:

> Mark this release as "Latest" (default [automatic based on date and version]). --latest=false to explicitly NOT set as latest

This description is wrong. Per the REST API, `make_latest` **defaults to `true`** for newly published releases — i.e. the release is made the latest. The behavior described in the help text ("automatic based on date and version") is actually what the `legacy` value does, not the default. So the help text is describing `legacy` behavior as if it were the default.

This should be corrected regardless of whether `legacy` support is added, and ideally would be updated alongside it.

### Proposed solution

Allow `--latest` to accept `legacy` in addition to `true`/`false`.

The [`--latest` flag](https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/pkg/cmd/release/create/create.go) is currently registered as a nil-able boolean:

```go
cmdutil.NilBoolFlag(cmd, &opts.IsLatest, "latest", "",
    `Mark this release as "Latest" (default [automatic based on date and version]). --latest=false to explicitly NOT set as latest`)
```

and serialized like this:

```go
if opts.IsLatest != nil {
    // valid values: true/false/legacy
    params["make_latest"] = fmt.Sprintf("%v", *opts.IsLatest)
}
```

Note the code comment already documents `true/false/legacy` as valid values, but because `IsLatest` is a `*bool`, `legacy` can never actually be passed.

A possible approach would be to change the flag from a nil-able bool to a string-valued flag (e.g. via `cmdutil.StringEnumFlag`) accepting `true`, `false`, and `legacy`, while still forwarding the value straight through to `make_latest`. Omitting the flag would keep the current behavior of not sending `make_latest`.

**Backwards compatibility must be preserved.** Any change should keep the existing usage working exactly as it does today:

- `--latest` (bare flag, no value) must continue to mean `true`.
- `--latest=true` and `--latest=false` must continue to behave as they do now.
- Omitting `--latest` must continue to leave `make_latest` unset (unchanged default behavior).

Only the additional `legacy` value would be new.

The same change would be relevant for `gh release edit`, which also exposes a `--latest` flag and should accept `legacy` consistently with `gh release create`.

### Additional context

- REST API reference: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/releases/releases?apiVersion=2026-03-10#create-a-release
- Relevant source: https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/trunk/pkg/cmd/release/create/create.go

title: 日本語ローカライズ対応の要望（macOS のアプリ別言語設定で日本語にできない）
body:
概要:
macOS の「アプリ別言語設定」で Copilot を日本語に設定しようとしましたが、「他の言語に対応していません」と表示され日本語化できませんでした。LANG=ja_JP.UTF-8 で起動しても表示は変わりませんでした。

再現手順:
1. システム設定 → 一般 → 言語と地域 → アプリ を開く
2. 「+」で Copilot を追加し、言語を「日本語」に設定
3. Copilot を再起動

期待する挙動:
アプリが日本語のローカライズを提供し、システムまたは環境変数で日本語表示になること。

補足:
- 試した環境: /Applications/GitHub Copilot.app を使用（詳細な macOS/Copilot バージョン、ログは追記可）
- 対応案: 日本語翻訳ファイルの追加、アプリ内言語選択、または macOS のアプリ別言語を尊重する修正

ラベル案: enhancement, i18n, localization

title: add --enable-release-immutability flag to repo edit

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body: added the --enable-release-immutability flag to the gh repo edit command. this makes a separate api call to the immutable-releases endpoint since it is not part of the standard repository patch payload. fixes #13818.

title: Add named field columns to gh project item-list

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
Proposal: cli/cli#13816
Tracking issue: github/planning-tracking#3431

## What this does

Adds named field columns to `gh project item-list`:

```
gh project item-list <project-number> --owner <login> --field "Status"
```

- `--field <name>` (repeatable) resolves the field by name and adds a column showing each item's value for it, with no separate field-ID preflight lookup.
- `--field-id <id>` does the same by node ID. The two are mutually exclusive, and an unknown `--field-id` lists candidate fields as `name (id)` for symmetry with the name-based errors.
- Name matching is case-insensitive, mirroring the Projects v2 platform API.

## Relationship to cli/cli#13807

This builds on the same shared name-resolution layer as the `item-edit` PR cli/cli#13807. To keep the two PRs independently mergeable, each one adds its own copy of the small, self-contained field-resolver file `pkg/cmd/project/shared/queries/resolve_fields.go` (~90 lines: `ResolveFieldByName`, `ResolveFieldByID`, and their error types), with identical contents in both. Because the copies match exactly, Git merges them into a single file automatically, so the two PRs can land in either order without conflicts or duplicate-definition build errors. The item-list-only `FieldValueNodes.DisplayValue` helper lives in its own `field_display.go`, so this PR leaves `queries.go` untouched.

title: Feature Request: Add flag to toggle "Preserve this repository" (GitHub Archive) in `gh repo edit`
body:
### Clarification
GitHub allows public repositories to opt into the GitHub Archive Program (Arctic Code Vault) via the "Preserve this repository" checkbox under Data Services in the repository settings. Currently, there is no way to manage this setting via the `gh` CLI.

### Proposed Solution
Add a flag such as `--preserve-repository` to `gh repo edit` to allow users to programmatically opt a repository into the GitHub Archive Program.

### Additional Context
For users managing hundreds of repositories, manually opting each repository into the archive program via the UI is incredibly tedious. A CLI flag would enable scriptable inclusion in the archive program.

title: Feature Request: Add flag to toggle wiki collaborator restrictions in `gh repo edit`
body:
### Clarification
Currently, the `gh repo edit` command supports enabling or disabling wikis via `--enable-wiki`. However, there is no flag to toggle the wiki's restriction setting (i.e., the "Restrict editing to collaborators only" checkbox found in the repository settings).

### Proposed Solution
Add a flag (for example, `--restrict-wiki-editing=false` or `--allow-public-wiki-editing`) to `gh repo edit` that controls whether non-collaborators can edit the wiki. 

### Additional Context
When automating the creation and configuration of open-source repositories, maintainers often want to allow public contributions to the wiki. Currently, this requires a manual UI intervention for every repository created.

title: Feature Request: Add `--enable-release-immutability` flag to `gh repo edit`
body:
### Clarification
Currently, the `gh repo edit` command allows users to enable or disable various repository features like issues, wikis, and projects. However, it lacks a flag to toggle the "Release Immutability" setting (`Settings > General > Releases > Enable release immutability`).

### Proposed Solution
Add a flag such as `--enable-release-immutability` to `gh repo edit`. This would allow organization administrators and automation scripts to enforce release immutability across an estate of repositories without needing to manually click through the web UI for each repository.

### Additional Context
Release immutability is a critical software supply chain security feature. Being able to automate its enablement alongside other repository settings would greatly improve the security posture of bulk-managed repositories.

title: gh project item-edit/item-list: address fields, options, and items by name
body:
### Describe the feature or problem you’d like to solve

`gh project item-edit` and `item-list` currently require ProjectV2 **node IDs** for the project, item, field, and single-select option. Getting those IDs means extra `field-list`/`item-list` preflight calls and copy-pasting opaque IDs, which is especially painful for scripting and for agents driving `gh`. Every other `gh project` verb that touches items (e.g. `item-add`) already accepts a human-readable `[<number>] --owner --url` addressing scheme, so `item-edit` is the odd one out.

### Proposed solution

Add **additive, backward-compatible** name-based addressing that mirrors `item-add`:

```
gh project item-edit <number> --owner monalisa \
  --url https://github.com/monalisa/myproj/issues/23 \
  --field "Status" --value "In Progress"

gh project item-list <number> --owner monalisa --field "Status"
```

- Existing `--id`/`--field-id`/`--project-id` flags are untouched; name flags are opt-in.
- `--value` is dispatched by the field's data type; for single-select it resolves the **option name**.
- Name matching is case-insensitive, mirroring the Projects v2 platform API.
- Resolution happens before the mutation, so bad names fail loudly with candidate lists instead of `400 []`.

Benefits CLI users (no more ID spelunking for a one-line edit) and unblocks agent/automation workflows that address Projects by name.

### Additional context

- A working implementation already exists as a draft PR: #13807 (name resolvers + both command surfaces, ~two-thirds tests).
- Part of a broader effort to bring Projects automation to parity across `gh` and the GitHub MCP server; a sibling MCP change (github/github-mcp-server#2760) adds the equivalent resolution there, and the two are being kept semantically aligned.
- Internal tracking issue: github/planning-tracking#3431.

title: Auth does not switch hostnames automatically
body:
### Describe the bug

Using `gh auth switch` does not change the hostnames automatically. I have two accounts configured, both with different hostnames:

```
barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh auth status
github.company.com
  ✓ Logged in to github.company.com account liama (keyring)
  - Active account: true
  - Git operations protocol: ssh
  - Token: gho_************************************
  - Token scopes: 'admin:public_key', 'gist', 'read:org', 'repo'

github.com
  ✓ Logged in to github.com account worksofliam (keyring)
  - Active account: true
  - Git operations protocol: ssh
  - Token: gho_************************************
  - Token scopes: 'admin:public_key', 'gist', 'read:org', 'repo'
```

Working when using regular GitHub:

```
barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh auth switch           
? What account do you want to switch to? worksofliam (github.com) - active
✓ Switched active account for github.com to worksofliam

barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh issue list -R codefori/vscode-ibmi

Showing 30 of 98 open issues in codefori/vscode-ibmi

ID     TITLE                                                                                                                             LABELS               UPDATED           
#3349  ....
...
```

Does not work when swapping to use Enterprise instance:

```
barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh auth switch
? What account do you want to switch to? liama (github.company.com) - active
✓ Switched active account for github.company.com to liama

barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh issue list -R my/repo                  
GraphQL: Could not resolve to a Repository with the name 'my/repo'. (repository)
```

Does work if I force with `GH_HOST`:

```
barry@MacBookPro ~ % GH_HOST=github.company.com gh issue list -R my/repo

Showing 7 of 7 open issues in my/repo

ID     TITLE                                                                     LABELS                                   UPDATED             
#....
```

### Affected version

```
barry@MacBookPro ~ % gh version
gh version 2.89.0 (2026-03-26)
https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/tag/v2.89.0
```

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Have multiple hosts
2. Use `auth switch` between them
3. Successful use any command

### Expected vs actual behavior

To work with the correct host.

title: Add name-based resolution to gh project item-edit and item-list
body:
Proposal: #13816
Tracking issue: github/planning-tracking#3431

> **Companion PR:** The `item-list` read path ships as a separate, independently-mergeable PR — #13823. Both PRs add their own copy of the small `pkg/cmd/project/shared/queries/resolve_fields.go` file, with identical contents. Because the two copies match exactly, Git merges them into one automatically, so the PRs can land in either order without conflicts.

## What this does

Lets `gh project item-edit` address ProjectV2 fields, single-select options, and items by **name** instead of node IDs. Existing ID-based flags are fully backward compatible; the name flags are additive and optional.

### `item-edit` (new name-based mode)
Mirrors `item-add` addressing:

```
gh project item-edit <project-number> --owner <login> --url <issue-or-pr-url> --field "Status" --value "In Progress"
```

- `[<project-number>]` positional + `--owner` resolve the project.
- `--url` resolves the item within that project.
- `--field <name>` resolves the field; `--value <value>` is dispatched by the field's data type (text/number/date, and for single-select the value is resolved as an option **name**).

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e7dceb1-0e4f-4706-9d95-37570a56cdcd

### Mutual exclusivity (enforced in code)
Every name/ID pair fails clearly when both are set, never silently merged: `--field` xor `--field-id`, `--url` xor `--id`, and `--value` xor the typed value flags (`--text`/`--number`/`--date`/`--single-select-option-id`/`--iteration-id`). Name-based flags also require the project number argument. Invalid combos are rejected before any GraphQL request fires.

### Errors are self-correctable
Name resolution happens before the ID-typed GraphQL mutation, so a bad name fails loudly with candidate names rather than `400 []`. Six failure modes mirror the sibling MCP PR (github/github-mcp-server#2760): field-not-found, field-ambiguous, option-not-found, option-ambiguous, wrong-field-type, item-not-in-project — surfaced as human-readable stderr messages listing candidates (CLI convention; not JSON).

### Name matching is case-insensitive
Field and option names are matched with `strings.EqualFold` to mirror the Projects v2 platform API, which normalizes field names via a `name_slug` and downcases single-select option names before matching. Field names are case-insensitively unique on the platform; option names are not, so a case-only option collision still surfaces the `option_ambiguous` error.

## Backwards compatibility
The command previously marked `--id` as a required cobra flag; this PR removes that requirement so an item can also be addressed by `--url`, but `--id` remains required in effect via a runtime guard (one of `--id` or `--url` must be set). Existing `--id`-based invocations are unchanged.

## Out of scope / follow-ups
- **`item-list` named columns** — ships as a separate, independently-mergeable PR (#13823).
- MultiSelect and issue-field-value node types: the shared resolver dispatches option resolution by field data type so adding these later is additive, not a rewrite.
- Bounding/paginating large read payloads (github/planning-tracking#3332).

## Coordination with the sibling MCP PR (github/github-mcp-server#2760)
- **Case-insensitive matching:** this PR matches names case-insensitively (see above). MCP #2760's resolver currently does case-sensitive matching and should be aligned so both surfaces behave identically.
- **Read-path mutual exclusivity:** the companion `item-list` PR (#13823) enforces reject-both for its name/ID field selector. MCP #2760's read path currently silently merges `fields`+`field_names`; that should be aligned to the same reject-both behavior.

title: gh issue develop reports an error with slash in branch name
body:
### Describe the bug

When I use `gh issue develop` to create and link an issue development branch, or to link an existing branch, the command fails when the branch name contains a forward slash `/`.

```bash
gh issue develop <issue-id> --repo <repo> --name feat/xxx
```

```bash
failed to create linked branch: API returned empty branch name
```

However, when the branch name does not contain a forward slash `/`, the command succeeds.

### Affected version

Please run `gh version` and paste the output below.

```bash
gh version 2.96.0 (2026-07-02)
https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/tag/v2.96.0
```

macOS

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. Prepare a GitHub repository and create an issue.
2. Create a branch whose name contains a forward slash.
3. Run `gh issue develop <issue-id> --repo <repo> --name feat/xxx` with the correct values.
4. See the error.

### Expected vs actual behavior

Expected: the issue should successfully link to a branch whose name contains a forward slash.

### Logs

```bash
GH_DEBUG=true gh issue develop 7 --repo S-zhi/Subtitles-AI --name ai-feat/restrict-cors-7

Fetching issue #7 ⣾* Request at 2026-07-07 01:53:01.812899 +0800 +08 m=+0.145975585
* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
Fetching issue #7 ⣷* Request took 879.852916ms
* Request at 2026-07-07 01:53:02.725362 +0800 +08 m=+1.058450960
* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
Checking linked branch support ⡿* Request took 565.013916ms
Checking existing linked branches ⣾* Request at 2026-07-07 01:53:03.325929 +0800 +08 m=+1.659025918
* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
Checking existing linked branches ⡿* Request took 486.727ms
* Request at 2026-07-07 01:53:03.851086 +0800 +08 m=+2.184189210
* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
Resolving base branch ⣾* Request took 458.676916ms
* Request at 2026-07-07 01:53:04.344852 +0800 +08 m=+2.677961835
* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
Creating linked branch ⣷* Request took 872.470375ms
failed to create linked branch: API returned empty branch name
```

### Additional context

Hello, this is my first time submitting an issue. If there is any problem with this bug report, please let me know.

I think this may be similar to  #10857.
Thank you for taking the time to read this issue.

title: `gh issue create` errors after successful creation when issue type can't be set
body:
### Describe the bug

`gh issue create` shows an error message with "does not have the correct
permissions to execute `UpdateIssueIssueType`" - even though the issue was
created successfully. Occurs only when you dont have the right permissions on
the repo.

### Affected version

```bash
gh version 2.96.0 (2026-07-02)
```

### Steps to reproduce the behavior

1. gh issue create --repo <repo-with-issue-types> on a repo where you cant set the type
2. See the error

### Expected vs actual behavior

**Expected:** Issue created, no error message.

**Actual:** Issue created, then X operation failed... GraphQL: <user> does not
have the correct permissions to execute UpdateIssueIssueType.

### Logs

```bash
gh issue create --repo cli/cli

Creating issue in cli/cli

? Title (required) `gh issue create` errors after successful creation when issue type can't be set
? Choose a template 🐛 Bug report
? Body <Received>
? Issue type Bug
? What's next? Submit

X operation failed. To restore: gh issue create --recover /var/folders/n6/x5dgkb0j4mncg9_0l47pyzr80000gn/T/gh2543392225.json

GraphQL: LangLangBart does not have the correct permissions to execute `UpdateIssueIssueType` (updateIssueIssueType)
```

title: Add JSON output support to pr create

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
## Summary

Fixes #11247.

Adds `--json` and `--jq` support to `gh pr create` without taking over the existing PR-template `--template` flag.

## Details

- Returns `id`, `number`, and `url` from pull request creation so they can be exported as JSON.
- Adds a JSON flag helper for commands that need `--json` and `--jq` but cannot expose the output-formatting `--template` flag.
- Covers command parsing, JSON output, jq filtering, and acceptance flow for PR creation.

## Validation

- `go test ./pkg/cmdutil`
- `go test ./pkg/cmd/pr/create`
- `go test ./api`
- `env -i HOME="$HOME" PATH="$PATH" USER="$USER" TMPDIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}" TERM=xterm-256color go test ./...`
- `env -u NO_COLOR TERM=xterm-256color PATH="$(go env GOPATH)/bin:$PATH" make lint`

Note: a first `go test ./...` run in the local shell failed because the shell environment had `TERM=dumb`, `NO_COLOR=1`, and `PAGER=cat`; rerunning in a clean terminal-like environment passed.

title: `gh status` shows unrelated repository activity that does not appear in API debug output
body:
### Description

Running `gh status` shows PR activity from repositories that I do not recognize as repositories I am subscribed to or otherwise expect to appear in my status output.

### Affected version
```bash
❯ gh version
gh version 2.91.0 (2026-04-22)
https://github.com/cli/cli/releases/tag/v2.91.0
```

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
1. Run `gh status`
2. view output. In my example, the section of concern was: 

```text
Repository Activity
vercel/next.js#95485                         new PR
publify/publify_textfilter_code#154          new PR
publify/publify_amazon_sidebar#157           new PR
publify/publify_core#253                     new PR
chef/bento#1689                              new PR
publify/publify_core#252                     new PR
IBM/secrets-manager-management-python-sdk#2  new PR
IBM/secrets-manager-management-node-sdk#2    new PR
vercel/next.js#95483                         new PR
```

`vercel/next.js` in particular stood out because I do not know why activity from that repository is appearing here.

### Initial subscription check
I first tried to inspect my repository subscription directly by running `gh api repos/vercel/next.js/subscription`. The result was:

```bash
  {
    "message": "Not Found",
    "documentation_url": "https://docs.github.com/rest/activity/watching#get-a-repository-subscription",
    "status": "404"
  }
  gh: Not Found (HTTP 404)
  gh: This API operation needs the "notifications" scope. To request it, run:  gh auth refresh -h github.com -s notifications
```

I did not add the `notifications` scope because I did not want to change the authentication/permission state while investigating why the repository was appearing.

## API debug investigation

I then ran `gh status` with API debugging enabled and redirected the debug output to a file `GH_DEBUG=api gh status 2> /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt` and then ran the following commands one at a time: 

```bash
    grep -n -C 20 'vercel/next.js' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt
    grep -n -C 20 '95485' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt
    grep -n -C 20 '"vercel"' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt
    grep -n -C 20 '"next.js"' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt
```

All 4 produced no output.  So the complete API debug file contains none of:

```text
  vercel/next.js
  95485
  vercel
  next.js
```

even though `gh status` rendered `vercel/next.js#95485    new PR`


## Inspecting the debug output

I then ran `head -100 /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt`. This was useful because it showed the GraphQL query being made. 

The query included:

```bash
  fragment issue on Issue {
    __typename
    updatedAt
    title
    number
    repository {
      nameWithOwner
    }
  }
  
  fragment pr on PullRequest {
    __typename
    updatedAt
    title
    number
    repository {
      nameWithOwner
    }
  }
  
  query AssignedSearch($searchAssigns: String!, $searchReviews: String!, $limit: Int = 25) {
    assignments: search(first: $limit, type: ISSUE, query: $searchAssigns) {
      nodes {
        ...issue
        ...pr
      }
    }
    reviewRequested: search(first: $limit, type: ISSUE, query: $searchReviews) {
      nodes {
        ...pr
      }
    }
  }
```

with variables:

```json
{
  "searchAssigns": "assignee:@me state:open archived:false",
  "searchReviews": "review-requested:@me state:open archived:false"
}
```

Those searches appear to explain assigned issues and review requests, but they do not obviously explain the unrelated PRs rendered under `Repository Activity`.

I then checked how many GraphQL queries appeared in the debug trace:

```bash
grep -n 'GraphQL query:\|GraphQL variables:' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt
```

The result was:

```bash
    14:GraphQL query:
    46:GraphQL variables: {
      "searchAssigns":"assignee:@me state:open archived:false",
      "searchReviews":"review-requested:@me state:open archived:false"
    }
```

Only one GraphQL query and one set of GraphQL variables were shown.

### Other API requests made by `gh status`

I then listed all requests shown in the debug trace: `grep -n 'Request to' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt`. The result was:

```bash
  2:* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
  49:* Request to https://api.github.com/graphql
  98:* Request took 487.333µs
  100:* Request to https://api.github.com/notifications?all=true&participating=true&per_page=100
  111:* Request to https://api.github.com/users/<username>/received_events?per_page=100
  152:* Request took 313.1655ms
  305:* Request took 729.136166ms
  337:* Request took 1.136122292s
```

I also inspected the region around the `received_events` request: `sed -n '111,220p' /tmp/gh-status-debug.txt`. Nothing in that output revealed `vercel`, `next.js`, or PR `95485`.


### What seems unusual
I do not want to claim that either the notifications endpoint or the received-events endpoint is responsible, because the debug output collected so far does not establish that.

The important observations are:
1. `gh status` renders `vercel/next.js#95485` and other unexpected PRs under `Repository Activity`.
2. The same invocation, with `GH_DEBUG=api`, still renders those entries.
3. The complete debug output contains no occurrence of the repository owner, repository name, full repository name, or PR number.
4. The GraphQL query shown in the debug output appears limited to assignments and review requests.
5. The trace also shows requests to the notifications and received-events endpoints, but inspecting and searching the trace does not reveal where the rendered `vercel/next.js` entry came from.
6. I deliberately did not add the `notifications` scope during this investigation because I did not want to change the permission state while trying to understand the existing behavior.

## Question

Why is `gh status` showing `vercel/next.js#95485` and the other expected PRs under `Repository Activity`, and why can none of the repository names or PR numbers used to render those rows be found anywhere in the `GH_DEBUG=api` output from that same `gh status` invocation?

title: gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --disable-auto: base ref not updated
body:
## Describe the bug

`gh pr merge <n> --squash --delete-branch --disable-auto` on a
repo with `allow_auto_merge: false` dispatches the merge (PR is
marked merged in GitHub's DB, branch is deleted), but the base
branch ref (`refs/heads/main`) is NEVER updated. The squash
commit exists in GitHub's object store but the ref does not
advance.

This has been reproduced 5 times on the same repo
(MoonBark-Studio/thistletide-gd): PRs #164, #165, #166, #170
(and the reproduction on #166 was re-triggered a second time).
The recovery every time is `git push origin <squash>:main
--force-with-lease=main:<parent-SHA>`.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Have a repo with `allow_auto_merge: false` (private repo default),
   `allow_squash_merge: true`, `allow_merge_commit: false`,
   `allow_rebase_merge: false`, `delete_branch_on_merge: true`.
2. Open a draft PR off `main`.
3. Mark ready, then run:

```sh
gh pr merge <n> --squash --delete-branch --disable-auto
```

4. The command returns exit=0 within ~2 seconds.
5. `git fetch origin main && git rev-parse origin/main` — shows
   the SAME SHA as before the merge.
6. Meanwhile, `gh pr view <n> --json state,merged` shows the PR
   is merged and the branch is deleted.

## Expected vs actual behavior

**Expected:** After exit=0, `origin/main` advances by exactly one
commit (the squash).

**Actual:** The PR is marked merged in GitHub's database and the
source branch is deleted, but `origin/main` does not advance.
The squash commit exists (reachable via API) but the base
branch ref update never happens.

## Evidence

PR #166 on MoonBark-Studio/thistletide-gd:
- Merged at 2026-07-03T19:00:26Z
- Merge commit SHA recorded as `a18dfd459054`
- `gh pr merge` returned exit=0 immediately
- After merge: `git show origin/main:scripts/git-merge.sh` — not found
- `gh api /repos/.../git/trees/main` — empty `scripts/` dir
- Recovery: `git push origin a18dfd45:main --force-with-lease=main:<parent>`

## Workaround

`scripts/git-merge.sh` (added to the affected repo) wraps the
merge with a `git rev-list --count == 1` poll + force-with-lease
fallback. Required on 5 of the last 6 squash merges.

## Environment

- `gh` version 2.95.0 (Ubuntu, apt install)
- Repo: private, `allow_auto_merge: false`
- No branch protection rules on `main`

title: fix: preserve percent-encoded path in DisplayURL

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
## Summary

Fixes #13546

`DisplayURL` was using `u.Path` which is the unescaped version of the URL path. This caused branch names with special characters (like quotes) to be displayed incorrectly in the terminal when using `gh pr create --web`.

For example, a branch named `test-"quoted"-branch-pr` would display as:

```
https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/compare/...test-"quoted"-branch-pr
```

Instead of the correct:

```
https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/compare/...test-%22quoted%22-branch-pr
```

## Changes

Uses `u.RawPath` (when available) instead of `u.Path` to preserve the original percent-encoding from the URL.

title: Bump keyring operation timeout from 3s to 60s

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
The 3-second timeout doesn't leave enough time to respond to an interactive unlock prompt (password entry, biometric confirmation, etc.), causing gh to give up before the user can react.

Fixes #11005

Manually built the binary and confirmed the new 60s timeout takes effect as expected.

title: gh skill install: support a configurable default agent

labels.name: gh-skill
labels.description: relating to the gh skill command

body:
## Feature request

When running `gh skill install`, the `--agent` flag must be specified every time. There is currently no way to persist a preferred agent, so users who always target the same agent (e.g. `claude-code`) must type `--agent claude-code` on every invocation.

## Proposed solution

Add support for a configurable default agent via `gh config`:

```sh
gh config set skill.default-agent claude-code
```

Then `gh skill install <skill>` would use that value when `--agent` is not explicitly provided, instead of always falling back to the hardcoded `DefaultAgentID = "github-copilot"`.

## Why this is straightforward

The registry already has `DefaultAgentID` as a Go constant in `internal/skills/registry/registry.go`. The install command could simply read `gh config get skill.default-agent` and substitute that value before falling back to the hardcoded default.

## Workaround

Users currently need a shell alias:

```sh
alias ghsi='gh skill install --agent claude-code'
```

This is workable but fragile — it doesn't compose with other flags and isn't portable across machines without dotfile syncing.

## Related

- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` env var already exists for controlling the Claude Code install path, showing precedent for agent-specific configurability.

title: feat(auth): Better multi-account `gh` account usage #13768

labels.name: external
labels.description: pull request originating outside of the CLI core team

body:
FIXES #13768

Automatically select which authenticated account `gh` acts as based on local, API-free context (working directory and base-repo owner) instead of the single global active account. This addresses cli/cli#13768: multiple accounts on the same host (e.g. an EMU + a personal github.com identity) cannot be disambiguated by host, and `gh auth switch` mutates global state, which is unsafe for concurrent agents.

New `account_rules` config key (gitdir + owner maps) and an explicit `--account` / `GH_ACCOUNT` override. Resolution wraps the HTTP client's token getter and never mutates persistent state.

Token precedence: env token (`GH_TOKEN` etc.) > `--account`/`GH_ACCOUNT` > rules >
globally active account. When no rules or override are configured, behavior is byte-identical to before.

`gh auth status` now shows which account resolves for the current context, and GH_DEBUG traces resolution, so automatic switching is observable rather than silent.

title: Harden deployment workflow
body:
Note, this includes https://github.com/cli/cli/pull/13492 which replaces the old CLI site PAT with a proper GitHub App.

This PR moves us from using federated environment secrets of macOS signing to using gatewatcher. All production releases will now require gatewatcher approvals.

This also adds a dry-run mode in order to be able to confidently iterate on the deployment workflow itself. While working on this I realised that the macOS installer wasn't getting signed but that appears to have been the case before this work as well.

I've added a timeout to the jobs because I saw a few previous runs that had ran for 6+ hours because the Windows code signing was hanging when using the staging environment.
